result: If I could change the world in one way, it would be to instill a universal sense of empathy in every person. Empathy—the ability to truly understand and share the feelings of others—could bridge divides, reduce conflict, and foster cooperation on a global scale. It would mean people would naturally consider the impact of their actions on others, whether in personal interactions, societal structures, or environmental decisions. Wars might lessen, inequality could be addressed with genuine care, and even small daily cruelties could fade. It wouldn’t erase differences or challenges, but it would give humanity a common foundation to build from. That’s the change I’d choose.
